senses.definition: The act of using something or putting it into practical use.
examples: The careful adoperation of new methods improved the results.
The report discussed the adoperation of modern tools in agriculture.

senses.definition: To legally take another person's child into your family and raise them as your own.
examples: They decided to adopt a baby from abroad.
The couple are hoping to adopt next year.

senses.definition: To start to use a particular idea, method, or way of behaving.
examples: The company will adopt a new marketing strategy.
More schools are adopting online learning platforms.

senses.definition: To choose a particular attitude, position, or style.
examples: He adopted a serious tone during the meeting.
They adopted a positive attitude toward change.

senses.definition: The qualities that make something suitable to be chosen or adopted.
examples: The different designs have varying adoptabilities for large companies.
We must consider the adoptabilities of these proposals before deciding.

senses.definition: The quality of being suitable or attractive enough to be adopted, chosen, or accepted.
examples: The adoptability of the new system depends on its cost.
Good user experience increases a product's adoptability.

senses.definition: Able to be adopted legally as a child or animal.
examples: The shelter has many adoptable dogs and cats.
She works to find homes for adoptable children.

senses.definition: Suitable to be taken up, used, or accepted.
examples: Only a few of the proposed measures are truly adoptable.
We need an adoptable solution that works in all regions.

senses.definition: A person who adopts a child, taking them legally as their own.
examples: The adoptant must show they can provide a stable home.
Both adoptants attended the court hearing.

senses.definition: Relating to or connected with adoption.
examples: The adoptative relationship was recognized by the court.
He acquired certain rights through an adoptative act.

senses.definition: Legally taken into another family and raised as their child.
examples: She is the adopted daughter of a famous actor.
He found his adopted family very supportive.

senses.definition: Chosen or accepted, especially as a way of life, country, or policy.
examples: He feels at home in his adopted country.
We must follow the adopted procedures carefully.

senses.definition: In a way that is adopted, chosen, or accepted.
examples: The policy was adoptedly followed by all departments.
These rules were adoptedly applied throughout the project.

senses.definition: A person, especially a child, who has been adopted.
examples: As an adoptee, she later searched for her birth parents.
Support groups for adult adoptees are growing.

senses.definition: People, especially children, who have been adopted.
examples: The charity offers counseling for adoptees and their families.
Many adoptees share similar questions about their origins.

senses.definition: A person or organization that adopts a child or animal.
examples: Each adopter was carefully checked before taking a pet home.

senses.definition: A person or organization that starts to use a new product, idea, or technology.
examples: Early adopters often pay more for new gadgets.

senses.definition: People or organizations that adopt children or animals.
examples: The agency offers training for new adopters.

senses.definition: People or organizations that begin to use a new product, idea, or technology.
examples: Early adopters helped the app become popular.
The report lists the main adopters of green energy solutions.

senses.definition: A person who supports the Christian doctrine that Jesus became God's Son by adoption.
examples: The council condemned the views of the adoptians.
An adoptian argued that Jesus was chosen because of his perfect obedience.

senses.definition: The Christian belief that Jesus was a human who was later adopted by God as His Son.
examples: Adoptianism was declared heretical by the early church.
The article explores the rise of adoptianism in medieval theology.

senses.definition: Different forms or expressions of the belief that Jesus became God's Son by adoption.
examples: The book compares various adoptianisms across Christian history.
Scholars distinguish between early and later adoptianisms.

senses.definition: A supporter or teacher of the belief that Jesus was adopted by God as His Son.
examples: The bishop was labeled an adoptianist by his opponents.

senses.definition: Supporters or teachers of the belief that Jesus was adopted by God as His Son.
examples: The council met to deal with the teachings of the adoptianists.

senses.definition: Taking a child or animal into your home as a permanent family member.
examples: They are thinking about adopting a child.

senses.definition: Starting to use a particular idea, method, or way of doing something.
examples: More companies are adopting remote work policies.
The school is adopting a new teaching method.

senses.definition: The legal process of making another person's child part of your family.
examples: They are going through the adoption process.
Adoption can be a long and emotional journey.

senses.definition: The act of starting to use a new idea, method, or product.
examples: The adoption of solar energy is increasing.

senses.definition: Relating to or connected with adoption.
examples: The judge discussed several adoptional issues in the ruling.
Adoptional questions arise in cross-border family cases.

senses.definition: The Christian belief that Jesus was a human being who was later adopted by God as His Son.
examples: Adoptionism challenged traditional views of Jesus' divine nature.
The council met to condemn adoptionism as heresy.

senses.definition: Different versions or interpretations of the belief that Jesus was adopted by God as His Son.
examples: Scholars study the various adoptionisms that arose in the early church.

senses.definition: A person who supports the belief that Jesus was adopted by God as His Son.
examples: The council declared the leading adoptionist a heretic.

senses.definition: People who support the belief that Jesus was adopted by God as His Son.
examples: The synod met to deal with the teachings of the adoptionists.

senses.definition: Instances of legally taking another person's child into your family.
examples: International adoptions have increased in recent years.

senses.definition: Cases where new ideas, methods, or products begin to be used.
examples: Corporate adoptions of cloud services are rising.

senses.definition: Connected with or arising from adoption.
examples: The court considered certain adoptious rights in the dispute.

senses.definition: Relating to the act of adopting or to people who have adopted a child.
examples: She grew up with her adoptive parents.

senses.definition: Relating to something that has been chosen or taken as your own.
examples: He loves his adoptive city.
English became her adoptive language.

senses.definition: In a way that relates to adoption or adoptive relationships.
examples: They are adoptively connected rather than biologically related.
The law treats adoptively acquired children the same as birth children.

senses.definition: Takes a child or animal into the family, making it a permanent member.
examples: She adopts a child every few years through the foster system.
The family adopts stray dogs from the shelter.

senses.definition: Begins to use a new idea, method, or behavior.
examples: The company adopts new safety standards every year.
He adopts a more careful approach after the accident.

senses.definition: A rare or obsolete form related to the verb 'adore' or the noun 'odor', not used in modern standard English.
examples: The term 'ador' appears only in a few historical texts.
Most learners will never encounter the word 'ador' in everyday reading.

senses.definition: The qualities that make people or things very cute and easy to love.
examples: The kittens' adorabilities were impossible to resist.
Different cultures may value different adorabilities in pets.

senses.definition: The quality of being very cute, attractive, and easy to love.
examples: Her adorability made everyone smile.
The puppy's adorability helped it find a home quickly.

senses.definition: Very cute, attractive, and easy to love.
examples: What an adorable baby!
Those puppies are absolutely adorable.

senses.definition: Extremely good or excellent, especially in a playful or slangy way.
examples: That was an adorable idea for a party.
Your new dress is simply adorable.

senses.definition: The state of being adorable and very easy to love.
examples: His shy smile added to his adorableness.
The film's adorableness won over the audience.

senses.definition: Different ways or instances in which someone or something is adorable.
examples: The various adorablenesses of the children made everyone laugh.

senses.definition: In a way that is very cute and easy to love.
examples: The baby smiled adorably.

senses.definition: Located near or toward the mouth, especially in some animals.
examples: The adoral tentacles help the animal catch food.
Scientists described a ring of adoral structures around the mouth.

senses.definition: In a position or direction near the mouth, especially in animals.
examples: The cilia move food adorally toward the mouth.

senses.definition: Showing worship or deep adoration, especially in religious art or writing.
examples: The painting shows an adorant figure kneeling before the altar.

senses.definition: A person or figure who worships or shows deep adoration.
examples: In the sculpture, the adorant leans forward in prayer.
Each adorant holds a small candle.

senses.definition: People or figures who worship or show deep adoration, especially in religious art or texts.
examples: The fresco shows a row of adorantes before the throne.
The hymn calls the faithful adorantes to gather.

senses.definition: Strong love and deep respect for someone or something.
examples: His adoration for his grandparents was clear.

senses.definition: The act of worshipping a god or holy thing.
examples: The church holds an hour of silent adoration each week.
Adoration of the sacred image is part of the ceremony.

senses.definition: Acts or expressions of strong love or worship.
examples: Her letters were filled with adorations and praise.

senses.definition: Expressing or showing adoration or worship.
examples: He gave her an adoratory glance.

senses.definition: To love someone very much.
examples: She adores her grandchildren.

senses.definition: To like something very much.
examples: I adore this song.

senses.definition: To worship a god or holy thing.
examples: They gather to adore the deity.
In this tradition, people adore several gods.

senses.definition: Very much loved or admired.
examples: He was an adored teacher at the school.

senses.definition: Someone who admires or loves another person very much.
examples: The singer was surrounded by a crowd of adorers.

senses.definition: Someone who worships a god or holy thing.
examples: The temple was filled with adorers.

senses.definition: People who admire or love someone very much.
examples: The actor smiled at his adorers.

senses.definition: People who worship a god or holy thing.
examples: Adorers gathered at the shrine.
The church remained open all night for adorers.

senses.definition: Loves someone very much.
examples: She adores her little brother.
He clearly adores his wife.

senses.definition: Likes something very much.
examples: He adores chocolate.
She adores going to the theater.

senses.definition: A scientific name for a group of beetles, often called rose beetles, found mainly in Asia and the Pacific.
examples: Farmers are concerned about damage caused by Adoretus species.
Adoretus versutus is known as the rose beetle in many regions.
